key_insight: Data mix matters as much as scale: different domains can interact synergistically or interfere, and those interactions can be quantified and predicted.
engineering_relevance: For language-model training, this suggests choosing pretraining mixtures strategically can improve benchmark performance more than adding more data from a single domain.
method: Estimate direct domain-to-benchmark synergy from observed variation across open-weight LLM pretraining mixtures.
Estimate second-order domain-domain synergy to capture effects that only appear when domains co-occur.
Use the learned synergy terms to predict optimal and anti-optimal data mixtures and compare against domain-agnostic scaling laws.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports improved predictive accuracy over domain-agnostic scaling laws and validation by training models on predicted optimal versus anti-optimal mixtures, where the estimated rankings matched observed performance.
watch_item: The approach relies on observational variation across existing models, so estimated synergies may be sensitive to confounding from training recipes, data quality, or unobserved hyperparameter differences.
paper_title: Domain-Aware Scaling Laws Uncover Data Synergy
